ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employee_performance" (
  "imp_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the employee */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the team the employee belongs to */,
  "turnover" INTEGER /* Turnover indicator (1: Yes, 0: No) */,
  "gender" INTEGER /* Gender of the employee (1: Male, 0: Female) */,
  "empowerment_score" REAL /* Score representing the level of empowerment perceived by the employee */,
  "lmx_score" REAL /* Leader-Member Exchange score for the employee */,
  "job_satisfaction" INTEGER /* Overall job satisfaction rating (on a scale) */,
  "climate_score" REAL /* Organizational climate score (on a scale) */,
  "cohesion_score" REAL /* Team cohesion score (on a scale) */,
  "engagement_level" INTEGER /* Engagement level of the employee (on a scale) */,
  "performance_rating" REAL /* Performance rating of the employee (on a scale) */,
  "promotion_eligibility" INTEGER /* Promotion eligibility indicator (1: Yes, 0: No) */,
  "training_needs" INTEGER /* Training needs indicator (1: Yes, 0: No) */,
  "career_goals" TEXT /* Career goals and aspirations of the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("imp_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_performance_team_id FOREIGN KEY ("team_id") REFERENCES teams ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the team */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the team belongs */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the manager of the team */,
  "team_size" INTEGER /* Number of employees in the team */,
  "team_type" TEXT /* Type of team (e.g., project-based, functional) */,
  "team_description" TEXT /* Description of the team's responsibilities */,
  "team_goals" TEXT /* Goals and objectives of the team */,
  "team_objectives" TEXT /* Key performance indicators for the team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the employee */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the employee */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the employee belongs */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date of hire */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* Employment status (e.g., full-time, part-time, contractor) */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the manager of the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES managers ("manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "managers" (
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each manager */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the manager */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the manager */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the manager belongs */,
  "management_style" TEXT /* Management style of the manager (e.g., autocratic, democratic) */,
  "leadership_experience" INTEGER /* Number of years of leadership experience */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance review */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the employee being reviewed */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date of the performance review */,
  "review_rating" REAL /* Rating given to the employee (on a scale) */,
  "review_comments" TEXT /* Comments provided during the review */,
  "goals_and_objectives" TEXT /* Goals and objectives set for the employee */,
  "development_plan" TEXT /* Development plan for the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reviews_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "surveys" (
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survey response */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the employee who responded to the survey */,
  "survey_date" TEXT /* Date the survey was completed */,
  "survey_type" TEXT /* Type of survey (e.g., engagement, satisfaction) */,
  "responses" TEXT /* Responses provided by the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("survey_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_surveys_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)
